name: D&D 5e

conditions.label: Blinded
conditions.description: Can't see, automatically fails sight-based checks. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage; its attack rolls have disadvantage.

conditions.label: Charmed
conditions.description: Can't attack the charmer or target them with harmful abilities. The charmer has advantage on social checks against the creature.

conditions.label: Deafened
conditions.description: Can't hear, automatically fails hearing-based checks.

conditions.label: Frightened
conditions.description: Disadvantage on ability checks and attacks while the source of fear is in sight; can't willingly move closer to it.

conditions.label: Grappled
conditions.description: Speed becomes 0 and can't benefit from bonuses to speed.

conditions.label: Invisible
conditions.description: Impossible to see without special sense; heavily obscured for hiding purposes. Attacks against it have disadvantage; its attacks have advantage.

conditions.label: Paralyzed
conditions.description: Incapacitated, can't move or speak, automatically fails STR/DEX saves. Attacks against it have advantage and hits within 5ft are critical.

conditions.label: Petrified
conditions.description: Transformed to stone, incapacitated, unaware of surroundings. Resistant to all damage; immune to poison and disease.

conditions.label: Poisoned
conditions.description: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

conditions.label: Prone
conditions.description: Only movement option is to crawl. Disadvantage on attack rolls; attacks against it have advantage if attacker is within 5ft, else disadvantage.

conditions.label: Restrained
conditions.description: Speed becomes 0. Disadvantage on attack rolls and DEX saves; attacks against it have advantage.

conditions.label: Stunned
conditions.description: Incapacitated, can't move, can speak only falteringly. Automatically fails STR/DEX saves; attacks against it have advantage.

conditions.label: Unconscious
conditions.description: Incapacitated, can't move or speak, unaware of surroundings, drops what it's holding and falls prone. Automatically fails STR/DEX saves; attacks against it have advantage and hits within 5ft are critical.

conditions.label: Exhaustion
conditions.description: Accumulates in levels 1-6, each level worsening effects (disadvantage on checks, speed halved, and more) up to death at level 6.

deathMechanicDescription: At 0 HP, roll a d20 at the start of each turn: 10+ is a success, below 10 is a failure. Three successes stabilizes; three failures means death.

restTypes.label: Short Rest
restTypes.description: Spend Hit Dice to recover HP; recharge short-rest features.

restTypes.label: Long Rest
restTypes.description: Full HP, all spell slots, and long-rest features restored.
